meta.commit: debb398 (worktree fast-forwarded from 7f00afb -> debb398 to reach Phase 0f)
regeneration_commands: SPAGHETTIO_DUMP_SNAPSHOTS=1 cargo test --manifest-path crates/core/Cargo.toml --test e2e -- --ignored census_science_pack_snapshots --nocapture --test-threads=1
methodology_caveats: Ground-truth pole positions only -- place_poles' decision order is NOT replayed (explicitly out of scope; a prior replay attempt diverged unexplained per the RFP).

Slack ('alternatives') is therefore computed against FINAL occupancy (all entities including every OTHER real pole, treated as pre-existing obstacles), not true decision-time state. This is a conservative/pessimistic proxy that can only OVER-count zero-slack poles relative to live decision-time instrumentation (Phase 2) -- never under-count. Same bias direction the pre-0f census carried.

Two slack windows reported per pole: local_slack (the pole's own ring only, x in px-3..px+3, 6 alternatives max) and band_slack (all rings of its matched band, same x window, up to 27 alternatives max). local_slack is the closer analogue to the pre-0f Motivation section's single-probe figures.

Row/band assignment: a pole or inserter's y is matched to the row+band whose pole_candidate_ys-derived band list contains it, preferring smallest ring-index d then smallest |y-top_y|. Seed rows (d=0) are provably unique per row (rows cannot overlap in y); outward rings (d=1..3) can rarely tie between adjacent rows when the inter-row gap is small -- see diagnostics.ambiguous_pole_row_band_matches.
within/beyond-inserter-span classification is census-defined (as it was pre-0f), not an engine fact: within/beyond the x-range of REAL inserters sitting exactly on that band's seed row.
Fluid vs solid row classification comes from the embedded solver output (MachineSpec.inputs/outputs.is_fluid) keyed by recipe, not the internal (unserialized) RowKind enum.
Poles are modelled as single-tile obstacles in the occupancy set, matching place_poles' own internal collision check (`placed.insert((px,py))`) -- NOT the pole's real 2x2 Factorio footprint.

pre_0f_comparison.note: pre-0f used a single-band probe window; post-0f local_slack is the closest analogue (same-ring x in px-3..px+3) but is NOT the identical formula -- see methodology caveats.

part_b_slack_distribution.HEADLINE_FINDING: Solid-row zero-local-slack poles: 18 (pre-0f: 0/1881). This is the literal condition of Phase 3 trigger (a) ('any solid-row zero-slack pole appearing in the Phase 2 scoreboard... currently zero, so any appearance is signal'). All instances are on advanced-circuit south-band (output-inserter row) poles -- see solid_zero_slack_detail. NOTE: this census cannot replay decision order, so these are computed against FINAL occupancy (all other real poles treated as pre-existing obstacles) -- a conservative/pessimistic proxy that may overcount relative to true decision-time slack. Phase 2's live instrumentation is the authoritative check the RFP already designed for exactly this reason.

part_c_pole_totals.reconciliation_note: The Phase-0f-landed decision-log entry cites a narrower/differently-scoped 'committed corpus' figure (1770 -> 3464, +96%) plus spot examples (EC@10 30->59, PU 163->331, util-sci 134->278) that do not exactly reconcile against either this census's full 45-case corpus (2212 -> 4226, +91%) or the individual case numbers here (e.g. tier5_processing_unit_from_ore_am3 here is 181->343, not 163->331; census_utility_science_pack here is 152->278 pre-0f, not the cited 134 -- though the post-0f figure of 278 matches exactly). 'EC@10' does not correspond to any of the 45 committed case names. Likely explanation: the decision-log figures were pulled from a different/uncommitted measurement pass during 0f's own development, not this census's 45-case corpus. Flagged as a blind spot, not silently reconciled.

part_e_fluid_row_budgets.note: free_tiles_both_bands_full_row / window_tiles_both_bands_full_row is the whole-row, both-band, all-ring free-tile budget within the shared pole_candidate_ys geometry -- the quantity a Phase 1 reservation formula would need to compare against coverage requirements (machine footprint x inserter count). All worst-case fluid rows in this corpus are basic-oil-processing / oil-refinery rows.

diagnostics.row_count_delta_note: 393 post-0f vs 401 pre-0f rows (-8, -2%) despite Phase 0f's own review claiming non-pole entities are byte-identical to base except pentapod (whose delta here is 0). Since row grouping here is independently recomputed from machine (y, height) pairs (the pre-0f census's row-grouping script is not committed and unavailable for diffing), this is most likely a row-counting methodology difference between the two independently written census scripts, not a real engine regression -- flagged, not silently absorbed.
